issue_title: Upgrade registry to docker-registry v2 (Docker Distribution)
issue_body:
There are several issues relating to registry performance (#3580, #3479).

We should upgrade to the Go-based rewrite of the [official Docker registry](https://docs.docker.com/registry/), which is now called [Docker distribution](https://github.com/docker/distribution). We'll also need to patch in our changes to support the `deis pull` workflow.

Additionally, there is also a [native driver](https://github.com/docker/distribution/blob/master/registry/storage/driver/rados/rados.go) to consume Ceph RADOS which is likely going to be significantly more performant than our current S3-on-Ceph implementation. We should investigate this, although it is likely to complicate our upgrade path.

Upgrading to registry v2 also allows us to support Docker 1.6-built images, resolving issues like #3550.

issue_title: Etcd staged startup
issue_body:
With etcd running inside of Kubernetes, we need etcd to be fault resilient and able to move from one host node to another.

To accomplish this:

- Discovery needs to be well done. Right now, the plan is to use an internal discovery service
- When pods come online, they need to first check to make sure that the old version of that pod is not still joined to the cluster. If it is, they need to remove and rejoin in order to get data.
- We need to check for race conditions in rapid restarts or in bringing a new pod online at the same time that an old pod is recovering. I believe the discovery service will handle most of this for us.

issue_title: deis/contrib/gce/gce-user-data generates an invalid cloud-config
issue_body:
On `deis 1.11.0`  when running 
```bash
./contrib/gce/create-gce-user-data $(curl -s https://discovery.etcd.io/new)
``` 
the cloud-config file generated is always invalid due to a null discovery value.
This line (in `contrib/coreos/user-data.example`): 
```bash
discovery: #DISCOVERY_URL
````
is always replaced (in the output file `contrib/gce/gce-user-data`) by : 
```python
"discovery": !!null |-
      null
```
After some debugging, I noticed that the placeholder `#DISCOVERY_URL` was already replaced by `null`even before reaching the line (in `contrib/gce/create-gce-user-data`) :
```python
dump = dump.replace('#DISCOVERY_URL', url)
```
So I replaced `#DISCOVERY_URL` by `__DISCOVERY_URL__` in both `contrib/gce/create-gce-user-data` and `contrib/coreos/user-data.example` and it worked.

The problem seems to be coming from this code :
```python
configuration_coreos_template = yaml.safe_load(coreos_template)
```

issue_title: user-data: flannel configuration setup fails on any node but the first
issue_body:
This line in `user-data` is responsible for setting up flannel through etcd: https://github.com/deis/deis/commit/445dbaecfadb1e2690734413cc43f30abcb13b21#diff-be438a7ce6a159afe476e6cb80c59eddR53

All nodes trying to `mk` this etcd key = first node succeeding, others failing. Eventually flanneld fails to start and that in turn makes docker fail to start.

I have already fixed this for my production cluster by changing it like this: (adding the error-supressing `-`)
```
ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/etcdctl mk /coreos.com/network/config '{"Network": "10.244.0.0/16", "SubnetLen": 24, "SubnetMin": "10.244.0.0", "Backend": {"Type": "vxlan"}}'
```
`etcdctl set` would also work, but would overwrite any customization the user might apply.

PR coming later, this issue reminds me to do it.

issue_title: get_rnn_classifier does not have a softmax/sigmoid
issue_body:
The function get_rnn_classifier does not have a softmax or sigmoid. Because of which the outputs of the classifier will not be probabilities. 

```
def get_rnn_classifer(bptt, max_seq, n_class, n_tok, emb_sz, n_hid, n_layers, pad_token, layers, drops, bidir=False,
                      dropouth=0.3, dropouti=0.5, dropoute=0.1, wdrop=0.5):
    rnn_enc = MultiBatchRNN(bptt, max_seq, n_tok, emb_sz, n_hid, n_layers, pad_token=pad_token, bidir=bidir,
                      dropouth=dropouth, dropouti=dropouti, dropoute=dropoute, wdrop=wdrop)
    return SequentialRNN(rnn_enc, PoolingLinearClassifier(layers, drops))

```
The PoolingLinearClassifier has a relu in the last layer and no sigmoid.

issue_title: Update tests to use the latest version of ipfs, 0.4.12
ipfsapi and ipfs mismatch reports 404 when trying to shut down daemon
issue_body:
Related to approach used to close #241.

Using ipfs v0.4.9 and ipfsapi v0.4.2 (which aligns to ipfs v0.4.9), when the ipfs daemon shutdown button is pressed in the ipwb web UI, the play system reports

`404 Client Error: Not Found for url: http://127.0.0.1:5001/api/v0/shutdown?stream-channels=true`

and does not shut down the daemon.

There are currently catches in place to account for ipfsapi<0.4.2, which does not support the `ipfsapi.shutdown()` command, as the ipfs daemon to which it corresponds (<v0.4.9) does not support the `ipfs shutdown` semantics.

This mismatch between the daemon the and Python API is inevitable, so catch this error and run, throw the exception, and execute the logic to kill the process.

issue_title: Automatic in-place override without caching for targeted module or all modules
issue_body:
Hi,

I was considering peru for my project. It seem pretty nice. However, I couldn't find any 
quick way to get a subset of modules described in the *.yaml file cloned directly to
their target location with their *.git (or *.hg) sub folder so that I can interract with
them.

I saw there is an override command but one has to clone his git / hg repository in
a separate step. This separate step seems superfluous most of the time as
the url mentioned in the *.yaml is exactly where I want to clone/pull/push from.

As a first step, it seem to me that it would greatly help if I could simply do
`peru override add --all` and have real clone put at their respective target locations using original url
for every module listed in the *.yaml file (hg, git, svn, etc; only the simple cases
where there are no complicated rules).

As a second step, it would be nice to be able to add modules to groups so
that the override command could target one or many groups (e.g.: `peru override add --group mygroup`).

It could also be nice to have a way to force using override from the *.yaml file systematically.

With these options, one can develop a package and its dependencies under the same
working directory and have all syncing deferred to peru (sync would stop on error).

Would there be any difficulties implementing theses enhancements?

issue_title: Use `sha1sum` to detect changes
issue_body:
I have a workflow where I develop a 'guest' project that lives in a subfolder of a 'host' project. When I want to make changes in the guest, it is much easier and straightforward to not do them in the guest's original repository but right in the subfolder within the host; that way, a test run immediately shows me whether my updates work. I then use a diff/merge tool (`meld`) to merge all changes back into the guest's repository, from where I commit and push to remote. 

At that moment in time, the contentent of the referenced guest repo and the contents of the guest subfolder are 100% identical. Sadly, when I then do `peru reup`, `peru` will complain that 'imported files have been modified', which is sort of true and sort of false. It's true that I did touch those files, but their contents are bit-identical with the ones that peru will only import with `--force`. IMHO this is questionable behavior; if I want to do `cp A B` but avoid to overwrite changes, then, clearly, when `sha1sum A` equals `sha1sum B`, I can totally do or not do `cp A B` (the only difference being possibly the file dates on `B`). At any rate, it should *not* cause `peru` to abort as no content changes will be effected either way.

My suggestion is to first check both the new and the old files' `sha1sum`s, and never opt out when both are identical.

issue_body:
Enumeration parameters (wherein one of a discrete set of values are allowed for the parameter) are a common need for many tasks.

The Slicer CLI [XML spec](https://www.slicer.org/wiki/Documentation/Nightly/Developers/SlicerExecutionModel#XML_Schema) supports string, integer, float, and double enumeration parameters for CLIs (see [example](https://github.com/cdeepakroy/cnn-image-classification/blob/master/ClassifyImage/ClassifyImage.xml#L20-L32)). 

The lack of support for enumeration parameters in girder-worker task-spec is a limitation in correctly converting XML spec of Slicer CLIs with these parameters and auto-generating UI for them in the item_tasks plugin of Girder.

issue_title: Implement periodic fetching of OCSP responses in a cron job
issue_body:
In #950, I add OCSP stapling in Nginx using its own fetcher. However, this is best-effort and fails to staple a response on the first request after startup.

Instead, we should use [ssl_stapling_file](http://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_ssl_module.html#ssl_stapling_file), which allows us to prefetch an OCSP response for each certificate and store it in a file to be loaded by Nginx. This will increase reliability, which is very important since we would like to eventually be able to implement OCSP Must Staple.

The cron job that fetches the OCSP response should run about once an hour. Even though OCSP responses are currently only regenerated every three days, this ensures that if there is any downtime or slop, the server gets an updated version very soon after it is available.

in particular much prefers that debug and release libs have the exact same filename
to avoid major boilerplate around lib loading (in java/kotlin) and using prebuilts etc. (in
cmake scripts).

Would be preferable to add MSVC in IF block in top CMakeLists.txt e.g.
```
IF (MSVC AND (CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Debug"))
   SET(CMAKE_DEBUG_POSTFIX "d" CACHE STRING "Debug Postfix for lib, samples and tools")
...
```

issue_title: X3D support
issue_body:
The [wishlist](http://www.assimp.org/wishlist.txt) lists VRML as a format that may get support in the future.

VRML has been superseded by [X3D](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X3D), so instead of (maybe) adding support for VRML, X3D support should probably be added instead.

X3D is "widely" used for 3D on websites (widely in quotes, because 3D on the web is still pretty rare).

X3D is just XML, so it should be pretty easy to parse. It's also an open format, so specifications should be readily available.

I'm creating this issue mostly just so I can see if/when this gets implemented. So just another wishlist item. :smile:

issue_title: glTF 2.0: Default material is not applied to meshes without material reference
issue_body: If a mesh references no material, it is supposed to use the default material according to the glTF 2.0 specification. However, assimp uses the material with index 0, which is a the first material imported (and not the default material), hence meshes intended to use the default material will use the incorrect material.

issue_title: Open archived csv like zip or gz
issue_body:
# Overview

It's an arising idea that we could provide an ability to open archived files.

---

_From @mcarans_

Is it possible to read zipped csv (or zipped Excel) with Tabulator? I can see I can pass a format (eg. csv) and some classes have an option "allow_zip", so I tried with the url: https://mdgs.un.org/unsd/mdg/Handlers/ExportHandler.ashx?Type=Csv&Series=710
```
    stream = Stream(url, format='csv', allow_zip=True)
    stream.open()
```

This gave me the error:
`tabulator.exceptions.OptionsError: Not supported options "allow_zip" for scheme "https" and format "csv"`

I can work round it with this somewhat ugly looking code:
```
    openedurl = urllib.request.urlopen(url)
    with ZipFile(BytesIO(openedurl.read())) as my_zip_file:
        source = my_zip_file.open(my_zip_file.namelist()[0])
        data = 'text://%s' % source.read().decode()
        stream = Stream(data, format='csv')
        stream.open()
```

---

_From @avmohan_

Support for gzipped csv files would be nice. 

Equivalent to:
```python
import gzip
import csv
with gzip.open('values.csv.gz') as ifile:
  csv_reader = csv.reader(ifile)
  for row in csv_reader:
    pass
```

issue_title: Datetime row_data from gcloud.bigquery.query.QueryResults.fetch_data contain invalid syntax
issue_body:
`datetime` `row_data` of the following form are given by `gcloud.bigquery.query.QueryResults.fetch_data`:

`datetime.datetime(2016, 3, 30, 23, 28, 18, 64, tzinfo=<UTC>)`

This is invalid syntax. It looks like the problem is that `tzinfo` is being set using `pytz`, but that somehow `tzinfo` is passed the `pytz.UTC` object representation, `pytz.UTC.__repr__() = '<UTC>'`, instead of `pytz.UTC`, the object itself.

Steps to reproduce (given `query`, the results of which include TIMESTAMP data):
```
import gcloud
import pandas as pd

query_results = gcloud.bigquery.Client(<my project>).run_sync_query(query)
df = pd.DataFrame()
first = True
page_token = None
while page_token or first:
    if first:
        first = False

    row_data, total_rows, page_token = query_results.fetch_data(page_token=page_token)

    if row_data:
        try:
            df = df.append(pd.DataFrame(row_data))
        except:
            print 'row_data: {}'.format(row_data)
            raise ValueError
```

Conceivably this could be related to a TIMESTAMP / DATETIME change

issue_title: Does not support nested blocks
improve selection of config profile name when running tests
issue_body:
The default profile is currently controlled from both config files and the <APP>_DEFAULT_PROFILE environment variable.  

But, when we run tests, in keg.testing, `ContextManager` hard-codes the profile name as 'TestProfile.'  Furthermore, `CLIBase` and Click's `CliRunner` create the app without any explicit testing profile.  So, in reality, when doing CLI Tests locally, the DevProfile is likely being used.

Need to make this more consistent.

issue_title: Invalid byxxx values allowed in rrule constructor
issue_body:
The current value checking for `byyearday`, `bymonthday`, `bymonth`, etc, only validate that the combinations of `byxxx` rules don't lead to an infinite loop, however, they allow arbitrary values, e.g.:

```python
from dateutil.rrule import YEARLY, DAILY, rrule

rr = rrule(freq=DAILY, bymonthday=32, count=1)
print(list(rr))     # []

rr = rrule(freq=YEARLY, byyearday=377, count=1)
print(list(rr))     # []

rr = rrule(freq=YEARLY, bymonth=13, count=1)
print(list(rr))     # []
```

Actually running each of these takes a while, because the iterator will generate all the possible values before `byxxx` constraints and reject them if invalid, even though we know ahead of time that these constraints are invalid.

This can be fixed one of two ways, depending on what the RFC has to say about it:

1. We raise a `ValueError` when there are out-of-range value components if the RFC restricts all components to be valid values.
2. If the RFC explicitly says that you must accept invalid `byxxx` values, we add in a `_generator_empty` attribute that is set in the constructor, and if it's `True` just return immediately in the iteration step.
3. If the RFC makes no comment either way, we choose one of the above two (I am leaning towards `ValueError` because it's almost certainly a mistake, but that has API consequences that "empty generator" doesn't).

issue_title: TypeError: Tag: IMKAD_Perceel invalid (type not found). Bug in pysimplesoap?
issue_body:
I'm trying to use pysimplesoap to get information from a SOAP service from the Dutch land register ([full WSDL here](http://www1.kadaster.nl/1/schemas/kik-inzage/20141101/verzoekTotInformatie-2.1.wsdl)) with PySimpleSoap. Unfortunately it is a paid service, so it will be hard/impossible for anybody here to replicate. But maybe I can explain what the problem is. 

So far I managed to connect and request information about a specific property with the following code:

```
from pysimplesoap.client import SoapClient
client = SoapClient(wsdl='http://www1.kadaster.nl/1/schemas/kik-inzage/20141101/verzoekTotInformatie-2.1.wsdl', username='xxx', password='xxx', trace=True)

response = client.VerzoekTotInformatie(
    Aanvraag={
        'berichtversie': '4.7',  # Refers to the schema version: 
        'klantReferentie': klantReferentie,  # A reference we can set ourselves.
        'productAanduiding': '1185',  # a four-digit code referring to whether the response should be in "XML" (1185), "PDF" (1191) or "XML and PDF" (1057).
        'Ingang': {
            'Object': {
                'IMKAD_KadastraleAanduiding': {
                    'gemeente': 'ARNHEM AC',  # municipality
                    'sectie': 'AC',  # section code
                    'perceelnummer': '1234'  # Lot number
                }
            }
        }
    }
)
```

Because I set `trace=True` I get extensive log messages, and in those log messages I see a humongous xml output which includes all info which I request. BUT, I also get this traceback:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
    'perceelnummer': perceelnummer
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pysimplesoap/client.py", line 181, in <lambda>
    return lambda *args, **kwargs: self.wsdl_call(attr, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pysimplesoap/client.py", line 346, in wsdl_call
    return self.wsdl_call_with_args(method, args, kwargs)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pysimplesoap/client.py", line 372, in wsdl_call_with_args
    resp = response('Body', ns=soap_uri).children().unmarshall(output)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pysimplesoap/simplexml.py", line 433, in unmarshall
    value = children and children.unmarshall(fn, strict)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pysimplesoap/simplexml.py", line 433, in unmarshall
    value = children and children.unmarshall(fn, strict)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pysimplesoap/simplexml.py", line 433, in unmarshall
    value = children and children.unmarshall(fn, strict)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pysimplesoap/simplexml.py", line 380, in unmarshall
    raise TypeError("Tag: %s invalid (type not found)" % (name,))
TypeError: Tag: IMKAD_Perceel invalid (type not found)
```
as far as I understand, this means that the `IMKAD_Perceel` tag cannot be understood by the simplexml parser which I guess is because it could not read/find the definition of this tag in the wdsl file. 

I used [SoapUI](http://www.soapui.org/) to introspect the WSDL file, and I found an url to an .xsd-file in which I find a definition of the `IMKAD_Perceel`:
```
<xs:element name="IMKAD_Perceel" 
    substitutionGroup="ipkbo:IMKAD_OnroerendeZaak" 
    type="ipkbo:IMKAD_Perceel"
    />
```
The tag seams to be closing itself though. I guess this means the definition is empty. Am I correct in this? And if so, is this the reason that pysimplesoap thinks that `IMKAD_Perceel` is not defined? 

And most importantly: is this a bug in pysimplesoap, or is this something which I can somehow solve myself? All tips are welcome!

issue_title: Run cells in different threads
issue_body:
Hi,

Sometimes I run long code in some cell, and still want to be able to run small snippets (independant) in another cell. But I cannot because the cells are ran sequentially on the same kernel.

Maybe the execution of each cell could be threaded, and then this would be possible. I know there could be issues with the GIL, but in most cases it could work I think. Typical use case would be to perform multiple (independant) long computations in parallel, in different cells, without having to deal with `subprocess` and so on, which would be super user-friendly.

The ideal use case, but which would be a lot more difficult to implement, is to be able to perform a long-running computation in a cell, and start to study the results. For instance, if a cell fills a list with data, it could be useful to start plotting and reading the list elements in another cell, while the computation is still running.

I did not find many references to this, except a [SO thread](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/32081926/a-new-thread-for-running-a-cell-in-ipython-jupyter-notebook).

Thanks

issue_title: A better metadata toolbar
issue_body:
Hi, Notebooks powered by the IRkernel got a competitor https://blog.rstudio.org/2016/10/05/r-notebooks/ (cc @takluyver) i’m pretty sad that they choose to build their own ghetto instead of joining our awesomely growing garden of language-independent tooling.

but to the point: they have some nice features: a progress bar and actually easily editable metadata.

out way frankly sucks: hidden behind a huge bar that only holds a small button is a a plain text editor for a JSON blob, which isn’t even highlighted and riddled with internal metadata from plugins (e.g. “collapsed” and “trusted”)

my idea is to standardise some common metadata (apart from the already standard “trusted”), that appears in the [extensions latex template](https://github.com/ipython-contrib/jupyter_contrib_nbextensions/blob/master/src/jupyter_contrib_nbextensions/templates/nbextensions.tplx)

* hidden (this cell is hidden from nbconvert output, but still able to be run, and can be unhidden by clicking some UI)
* hide_input, hide_output (self explaining)

then we make that and nonstandard metadata available via a easy to use toolbar.

1. the bar only appears as bar and only increases cell height when it contains nonstandard cell metadata, which can then be graphically edited. (else it’s maybe just a small rectangle in the cell corner with a expand button)
2. the UI for metadata i imagine is a horizontal bar with <kbd>**key**: *value*</kbd> pairs that can easily be appended to or removed from. the values can easily be switched between JSON primitive types.
3. extensions can register top-level keys that they provide custom UI for, which takes the place of the normal *value* UI.

---

a minimum working version would be a expandable/retractable bar with inline JSON editor.

issue_title: Consider adding option to remove extra newlines created during cell merge
issue_body:
Merging cells in recent versions of Jupyter causes extra newlines to be inserted between the code in each cell. I don't think this was always the behavior, and find myself having to manually remove the extra spacing after every merge. 

I cannot find the part of the code that is causing this behavior. I thought that it was lines 1612 and 1616 in the `Notebook.prototype.merge_cells` function of [notebook/notebook/static/notebook/js/notebook.js](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/71c5057ae525f8a21b099c31bc6178046bbc5d5d/notebook/static/notebook/js/notebook.js#L1612-L1616), but when I modified this in my local copy to remove the two newline characters from the join, it didn't seem to have any effect.

I'm not asking that it be hard-coded one way or the other, but instead that it could be configurable in a ~/.jupyter configuration file so that people could set whichever behavior they prefer.

Thanks.

issue_title: Question: Is there a reason meta title and og:title are the same?
issue_body:
I've been recently implementing `django-meta` into a project and noticed that `<title>` and `<meta property="og:title" />` both use the same exact variable (`title`) as opposed to `description`, which has a specific, optional `og_description`, that falls back to `description`.

I'm wondering if there's intention behind this (maybe I'm unaware of a best practice?) or if a PR to making it more like the `description` property would be welcome?

Thanks!

issue_title: Change format of members datatable in aggregator
issue_body:
Right now the format of members datatable (`nrows` x 1) , that is generated and returned by the aggregator, is as follows:

- 1D and 2D: group id a member belongs to
- ND: exemplar id a member belongs to

When there are no exemplars in a particular bin or group, the format used for 1D and 2D cases is not consistent with the ND one. To make these two consistent, group ids should be converted to exemplar ids during the aggregation.

issue_title: Missleading docstring of 'between' function
issue_body:
The docstring of 'between' function in 'boto3/dynamodb/conditions.py' at line 118 is misleading.
The description of 'low_value' and 'high_value' parameters are incorrect according to AWS docs: http://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_Condition.html

issue_title: CloudFront: DistributionConfig.ViewerCertificate parameter validation does not match docs-defined spec
issue_body:
I think I found a bug with the CloudFront client's update_distribution method.

I'm trying to update my CloudFront Distributions to use an ACM-generated certificate. According to the docs, this ability is included in [1.4.0](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/cloudfront.html#CloudFront.Client.update_distribution) and uses a key called 'ACMCertificateArn'. 

However there seems to be a problem with the parameter validation on the ViewerCertificate object, as shown in this error:

```
botocore.exceptions.ParamValidationError: Parameter validation failed:
Unknown parameter in DistributionConfig.ViewerCertificate: "ACMCertificateArn", must be one of: Certificate, CertificateSource, SSLSupportMethod, MinimumProtocolVersion, IAMCertificateId, CloudFrontDefaultCertificate
```

I'm using version 1.4.0

issue_title: snaphot copy not using DestinationRegion
issue_body:
Between Boto2 and Boto3 the copy of the snapshot is completely different, and basically in Boto3 it's not possible to do a region to region copy.

If you want to execute a snapshot.copy(), you obviously need to access first the source snapshot, so why is it mandatory to provide the SourceRegion ?

then, as we speak, when using DestinationRegion, nothing is happening, and it seems that it's related to the PresignedURL only.

Is there any working sample of the snapshot.copy() working in Boto3 ? (in boto2 it's working, but using the copy_snapshot approach, which is different as you provide simply the source Region and source snapshot Id)

issue_title: s3 documentation missing parameters definition for upload_file
issue_body:
If you look at the documentation here, you'll see that while most methods have params defined in an html element named `docutils field-list`, `upload_file` does not.  Same is true for `download_file` and `get_waiter`.   But that's it; all the others have params defined.
1. if this is fixed then the `pyboto3` code generator will be able to properly process `upload_file` and `download_file` methods.
2. I am just curious what is different about the implementation of _these_ methods that causes them to not have full documentation.

issue_title: Invoking a Lambda function causes future calls to Queue.delete_messages to fail
issue_body:
We have a service that:
- reads messages from SQS
- invokes a lambda function based on the contents of each message
- deletes the message from SQS

It seems that invoking the lambda function causes the message to never be deleted, it remains "In Flight" until the visibility timeout ends and it gets put back in the queue.

```
import boto3
import json

sqs = boto3.resource('sqs')
_lambda = boto3.client('lambda')

queue = sqs.get_queue_by_name(QueueName='yyy')
sqs_response = queue.receive_messages(
    MaxNumberOfMessages=1
)
message = sqs_response[0]

response = _lambda.invoke(
    FunctionName='xxx',
    InvocationType='Event',
    LogType='None',
    Payload=message.body,
)

delete_response = queue.delete_messages(
    Entries=[
        {
            'Id': message.message_id,
            'ReceiptHandle': message.receipt_handle
        }
    ]
)
```

The above is a small example of our issue, commenting out the `_lambda.invoke` line causes the message to be deleted properly. Even though our response indicates it should have been deleted:

```
{u'Successful': [{u'Id': 'xxx'}], 'ResponseMetadata': {'RetryAttempts': 0, 'HTTPStatusCode': 200, 'RequestId': 'yyy', 'HTTPHeaders': {'x-amzn-requestid': 'zzz', 'content-length': '386', 'server': 'Server', 'connection': 'keep-alive', 'date': 'Wed, 23 May 2018 14:11:56 GMT', 'content-type': 'text/xml'}}}
```

- this seems to be an issue with not just `queue.delete_messages()` but also `message.delete()` as we have tried many different combinations

issue_title: How do I get the HTTP URL using Botocore?
issue_body: I've always used the Boto library to use Amazon's S3 service, but because of a Python 3.4 project I had to use Botocore instead. I've figured out how to do most things, but I can't seem to find how to do one (pretty essential) thing; generating urls. In Boto I would simply set a Key and fire the generate_url method. How do I do this in Botocore? I know how to download and save files, but I would much rather just get a link because of server performance and what-not.

issue_title: Support for Mediaflux S3
issue_body:
I'm working on a project backed by a non-AWS S3 installation. It uses Mediaflux S3 (http://www.arcitecta.com/Products/Mediaflux). That was not my decision, and I know this means AWS isnt getting paid ;-), but I'd like to still use boto3 as my client for this. Mediaflux is an Australian product and it likes to use commas in float values i.e "2345,67" instead of "2345.67" in its ContentLength headers. This causes a fatal error in boto3 and makes it impossible to use boto3 against Mediaflux. To resolve this, I successfully monkey-patched botocore.

Gist:
https://gist.github.com/sachsbl/f6619b1d735641233e6e6b68fbb5aa58

Wrapped-client implementation:
https://github.com/sachsbl/Mediaflux_S3

I would like to apply this patch to botocore itself so we can abandon my monkey-patch approach. I plan on opening a Pull Request with the small change required. Thanks!

issue_title: Use default value when value is None
issue_body:
Using the default keyword is convenient.
```python
Class Person(Model):
    name = StringType(default='Guido')

p = Person() 
print(p.name) 
>>> 'Guido'
```
However, if the value gets set to None, the default isn't triggered.
```
p = Person({'name': None}) 
print(p.name)
'None'
```
This makes it hard to abstract away the instantiation of a model.
Ideally the default value should always be set when the value is None.

issue_title: Problem with parse_smtlib2_string
bit2bool/mkbv functions in bitblasted floating-point formulas
issue_body:
_All tests conducted using Z3 4.5.1 64-bit (commit 3350f32e1f2c01c9df63b7d71899796a18ce2272, compiled with gcc  5.4.0) and Python 3 on a Ubuntu 16.04 system._


Hi all,

## Context

I want to "translate" floating-point formulas into CNF boolean formulas using the Z3 API. Here is my first attempt:

```python
from z3 import *

x = FP('x',Float16())
c = FPVal(1024,Float16())
formula = And(x < c, x > 0)
goal = Goal()
goal.add(formula)
# taken from https://stackoverflow.com/a/23932235
tacs = Then(Tactic('simplify'),Tactic('fpa2bv'),Tactic('simplify'),Tactic('bit-blast'),Tactic('tseitin-cnf'))

# track input bits
for i in range(16):
    b = Bool('b' + str(i))
    mapping = b == (Extract(i,i,fpToIEEEBV(x)) == BitVecVal(1,1))
    goal.add(mapping)
    
bitblasted = tacs(goal)[0]
for f in bitblasted:
    print(f)
```

The middle block (`for i in range(16)...`) maps a new boolean to each bit of the input ('x' in this example). The aim is to create a set of variables that, if used as a projection scope for a SAT model counter (e.g. sharpSAT), will make the model counter return the number of solutions for the original formula. 

## Problem

Here is a snippet of the output from the execution of the script above:

``` 
[...]
Or(k!36,
   bit2bool(k!19(mkbv(k!20, k!21,k!22, k!23,k!24,k!25, k!26, k!27, k!28,k!29, k!30, k!31, k!32, k!33,k!34,k!35))),
[...]
```

My expectation was that no functions (well, other than Or/And/Not) would appear in the bitblasted formula. Applying the same idea to bitvector variables directly (example code [here](https://gist.github.com/MateusAraujoBorges/cda62d905e2ae94f2b877450bd2d7478)) works as expected. Am I doing something wrong in the python code above? 

## Addendum

Executing the python code above with `rewriter.hi_fp_unspecified` set to `true` results in a segfault.

issue_title: [CMake] Regeneration of some `.cpp` files are broken
issue_body:
Currently the CMake build system fails to re-generate the `install_tactic.cpp`, `mem_initializer.cpp`, and `gparams_register_modules.cpp` files when the headers that are used to generate these files change.

The reason for this is because CMake is currently unaware that the generation of these files depends on certain header files.

We cannot use shell globing in CMake to fix this because this will fail in the case that new header files are added. The right way to fix this is to change the interface of `z3_add_component()` so that header files that need to be scanned to generate header files are explicitly listed.

When I have the time I'll fix this. This blocks #986.

issue_title: linke turbidity resolution should be 1/12 of an arcdegree
issue_body:
The Linke turbidity should be 1/12 = 0.0833 of an arc degree or 5' (minutes of arc degrees) - see [section 8 from "Aerosol optical depth and Linke turbidity climatology"][1] and [SoDa Pro: Linke Turbidity Factor](http://www.soda-pro.com/help/general-knowledge/linke-turbidity-factor), or the old [SoDa Linke Turbidity](http://www.soda-is.com/eng/services/linke_turbidity_maps_info.html) and [reference](http://www.soda-is.com/eng/services/linke_turbidity_info.html). Instead latitude is 180/2159 = 0.083372 and longitude is 360/4319 = 0.083353.

Section 8.1: Format of the data explains:

>Optionally the files are also available in 1/12° resolution. These files were organised in the following form:
1st row: 89.9583° S, 2nd row: 89.875 ° S, …
1st column: 179.9583°W, 2nd column: 179.875°W, …

This indicates that each measurement is at the center of the node, not the edges. _IE_: the 1st node is not at 90° but (90° - 1/24°) =  89.9583°.

The values of TL at points for any latitude along the longitude -180° and +180° don't match, and therefore indicate that they are not the same.

![lt_180lon_janjun](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1385621/20287215/1837a35e-aa80-11e6-92bf-f9233c6e15a4.png)

[1]: http://www.meteonorm.com/images/uploads/downloads/ieashc36_report_TL_AOD_climatologies.pdf

issue_title: Make a 2.2 release
issue_body:
We have a decent amount of new features and bug fixes for a new stable v2.2 release.  Some big features (embedding support seems the biggest), lots of minor ones, and various bug fixes have landed into `master` over the last four months.  I'm not suggesting we release it tomorrow, but I think we should start thinking about where to draw the line for 2.2.

PRs I think we should aim to merge:

- [x] #951, #952 are bug fixes (and ready to merge).
- [x] #805 (init factories) - the implementation is done; we need to decide whether to make this core (i.e. included by `pybind11.h` or not), and then I'll update the documentation accordingly before merging.
- [x] #949 (`py::local` support for keeping a type's registration local to the module).  This was a sort of experiment to think about fixing #919, but I think it worked out into a nice feature -- and I was actually surprised just how little code it actually needed to implement.  This still needs me to write up the documentation, but I'll do that in the next couple of days.
- [x] #928 (FindPythonLibs{,New} cmake compatibility) - this is ready to merge AFAICT.
- [x] #925 (bool and numpy bool conversions)
- [x] #907 would be nice to have it for the clearer command line instructions.
- ~~#901 (cutting off the maximum length in generated signatures) is a nice cosmetic feature.  It needs some more tweaking, but if that happens it seems worth including.~~ --> for v2.3
- ~~#934 (constexpr type signatures for C++11 and MSVC).  Currently has a minor merge conflict, but aside from that I think it's ready to merge.~~ --> for v2.3

Other features in the queue that I think might not make it:

- #864 (Revising type casters) - I think this is going to be a pretty significant change, and likely to go through a fair amount of discussion/suggestions/changes from start to finish.  I'm not opposed to including it if its done soon, but of course I also think it's important to get it right.
- #781 (python 3.4 enums) - this is still marked WIP and, I guess, still in progress.  I think the last discussion was that it was going to be separate from `enum_<>` due to backwards-compatibility issues with how `enum_` worked; beyond that I'm not entirely sure of the status.


Thoughts?

issue_title: [VM] Could the open-port command allow specifying a port?
issue_body:
The simplified `az network nsg rule create` command is awesome, however, it would be great if you could use the `az vm open-port` command as a VM-focused way of achieving the same thing, but without having to look up and manage the NSG explicitly (fewer concept counts for new Azure users would be great!).

Since `az vm create` auto-creates an NSG behind the scenes, being able to expose a port on the VM would help support an easier "getting started" experience with VMs. Instead of the `open-port` command exposing all ports, it would nice to do something like the following:

```shell
az vm create -g <group> -n <name> --image UbuntuLTS
az vm open-port -g <group> -n <name> -p 80
```

Even further, I would actually love if it the `az vm create` command allowed specifying a list of ports to expose on the resulting NSG. Something like the following would be amazing:

```shell
az vm create -g <group> -n <name> --image UbuntuLTS --expose-ports 80,443
```

Both of these help achieve the same goal of preventing a user from needing to think about the NSG until necessary. Since exposing a port is such a common operation, it seems like this would be a nice companion/extension of the already-great abstraction created by the `az vm create` command in the first place.

issue_title: [Lock] Creating locks seems like it could be simplified a little bit
issue_body: The lack of help text for the `az lock create` command makes it a little difficult (for me at least) to know what input it expects, even when being familiar with creating locks in the portal. For example, I wanted to lock a resource group, so I tried running the following command, assuming that the `--lock-type` arg was what I wanted, and guessing that `delete` was a valid option: `az lock create --name -g --lock-type delete`. This resulted in an `The lock '' request is invalid.` error, so I double-checked the portal, which seemed to match the usage I was trying to achieve (e.g. lock type = delete). I decided to create the lock via the portal and then ran `az lock list` to see what the lock's metadata looked like. Surprisingly, I noticed that it's `level` was set to `CanNotDelete` and it's `type` was set to `Microsoft.Authorization/locks`. So I tried running `az lock create` again, but this time setting the `--lock-type` argument to `CanNotDelete`, and that failed with the same error as above. I'm probably being super dense here, but it would be awesome if the `az lock create` command could add help text for each arg, provide the list of allowable values for enum args (e.g. `--lock-level`), as well as an example of what it looks like to create common locks (e.g. from deleting a resource group). Additionally, the only arg that is marked as required is `--name`, and I'm not sure if that's accurate or if `--lock-level` or `--lock-type` are also required? (unless they provide a smart default?). The portal seems to require a lock type, so if that's the case, it would be cool if that requirement could be made clear in the help text of the `az lock create` command as well. Finally, for ergonomic purposes, it would be cool if we could add short aliases for common args of this command (e.g. `-n` for `--name`, `-t` for `--lock-type`).

issue_title: [Discussion] Typing a command group should print its help text by default
issue_body: It would be great if typing a command group (e.g. `az component`, `az network`) would print out the same text that you get when appending the `-h` flag. This would make the already well-documented nature of the CLI feel a little more easy to navigate. For example, typing `az network` does in fact print out the list of acceptable commands, but it's a pretty long list, with no help descriptions, and so you really have to add `-h` to make that text usable. I've always felt like help flags are mostly used for commands (to indicate that you don't want to execute it), and not command groups, and many CLIs special-case executing a command group by printing out its pretty-printed help text. For example, if you run `docker network` (or any other group/sub group), you get the full help text, that is analogous to what you'd get by running `az network -h`.

issue_title: Open up the paging mechanism in list methods.
issue_body:
Take examples of [list vm](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/compute/v2017_03_30/operations/virtual_machines_operations.py#L635), and [list ad users](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-graphrbac/azure/graphrbac/operations/users_operations.py#L99). In both operations, the internal paging mechanism travels the entire results before returning.The mechanism results in a poor performance when the number of the results is large.

An ideal change is to return results as it fetches data. For example, the list methods can return a generator instead of a list, which allows the consuming application to output the result as the SDK querying the data so as to offer better user experience.

An ultimate solution is to provide server side paging API by allowing `top` and `count` parameter in list function.

issue_title: Move introspection APIs into hazmat
issue_body:
Now that we have a little more experience with how trio is used, I think I want to move the API that's purely for introspection into `hazmat`; this keeps the core namespace and core reference manual smaller and focused on regular implementation code.

Affected symbols:

* `current_clock`
* `add_instrument`, `remove_instrument` (no deprecation needed b/c these are new in 0.2.0 anyway)
* `current_statistics`

issue_title: v0.2.0 broken on Windows
issue_body:
Due to openssl limitations: https://github.com/pyca/cryptography/issues/3194

Need to reduce our cert validity period. How much? Not clear. [MSDN says](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/0z9czt0w.aspx) that "`_gmtime64`, which uses the `__time64_t` structure, enables dates to be expressed up through 23:59:59, December 31, 3000, UTC, whereas `_gmtime32` only represent dates through 23:59:59 January 18, 2038, UTC.", so probably one of those is the limit.

issue_title: Multiple CI platforms ?
issue_body:
We've just got a pretty nasty bug with CI on MacOS (see #37) that caused me time and frustration :cry: 
This lead me to wonder what is our policy to decide which platform should be CI tested.

In theory the more the better, however more platform means more exotic ci-specific code to support, more platform-specific crashes to debug (especially non linux platforms, which are often hard to reproduce locally) and more pain waiting for CI to finish ^^

I would say the gain outweigh the cost for libraries dealing with low level stuff like trio does (that's why I opened https://github.com/python-trio/trio/issues/498).
However for pytest-trio I fell like platform differences should be almost non-existent (given the codebase and our dependencies)

A middleground would be to keep multi-platform support, but keep it to it minimal (one windows and one macOS build with last CPython for instance)

issue_title: Release 1.1.18
Add native engine binary existence check to deploy script.
issue_body:
There was a bug (#4085) in the native engine auto-publishing code for OSX that prevented just the mac binaries from making it to bintray and this was silent where it should at least block a deploy.

Similar to the package ownership test done by the deploy script, a quick `HEAD` request for `linux/{i386,x86_64}` and `mac/10.{7-12}` should be done to confirm that the current native engine version - as given by the following is deployed to bintray:
```
./pants options --scope=native-engine --name=version --output-format=json | \
  python -c 'import json, sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)["native-engine.version"]["value"])'
```

issue_title: Build python binary with no source or entry point
issue_body:
I'm trying to build a pex file that I can use to execute external scripts like this.

```shell
./portable_venv.pex ./my_python_script.py
```

I can build the pex manually with the `pex` tool:

```shell
pex -i http://some_pypi_server.com/prod/release bsddb3 my-internal-library sh -o ./portable_venv.pex --disable-cache -vvv
```
What I would like to do is use pants to build this kind of binary for me, so I don't have to write custom wrapping around `pex`.

Here is my config below:

`<build_root>/src/pex-library/bin/BUILD`
```python
python_binary(
    dependencies=[
        'src/pex-library/lib:lib'
    ],
    entry_point='main',
    indices=[
        'http://some_pypi_server.com/prod/release/+simple/'
    ]
)
```

`<build_root>/src/pex-library/lib/BUILD`
```python
python_requirement_library(
    name='lib_requirements',
    requirements=[
        python_requirement('bsddb3'),
        python_requirement('sh'),
        python_requirement('my-internal-library'),
    ],
)

python_library(
    name='lib',
    dependencies=[
        ':lib_requirements',
    ]
)
```

When I build
```sh
$ ./pants binary src/pex-library/bin
...
Exception message: Invalid target PythonBinary(BuildFileAddress(BuildFile(src/pex-library/bin/BUILD, FileSystemProjectTree(/home/user/src/pants)), bin)): A python binary target must specify either source or entry_point.
```

`pex` doesn't require `source` or `entry_point` and still builds a pex for me (probably because of a good default), how can I achieve this result with a pants BUILD?

According to `src/python/pants/backend/python/targets/python_binary.py`:
```python
 44     :param source: relative path to one python source file that becomes this
 45       binary's __main__.
 46       If None specified, drops into an interpreter by default.
 47     :param string entry_point: the default entry point for this binary.  if None, drops into the entry
 48       point that is defined by source. Something like
 49       "pants.bin.pants_exe:main", where "pants.bin.pants_exe" is the package
 50       name and "main" is the function name (if ommitted, the module is
 51       executed directly, presuming it has a ``__main.py__``).
```
^ says `None` is a valid value, but we are explicitly checking here (same source file).
```python
  8     if source is None and entry_point is None:
  7       raise TargetDefinitionException(self,
  6           'A python binary target must specify either source or entry_point.')
```

Please help :).

Thank you,
/me

issue_title: Code generators (like AutoValue) can leave stale .class files lying around in classpath
issue_body:
In this release:

 https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2451/

Stu added support for automatically sucking in the .class file output from code generators like AutoValue.

But there is an issue with stale files that needs to be addressed.  Here is a repro:

```
$ ./pants clean-all

$ ./pants compile examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue --compile-zinc-java-enabled

$ find .pants.d -name '*Auto*.class' -ls
180369417        8 -rw-r--r--    1 zundel           zundel               1185 Jul 13 11:12 .pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/AutoValue_AutoValueMain_ValueType.class
180369415        8 -rw-r--r--    1 zundel           zundel                622 Jul 13 11:12 .pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/AutoValueMain$ValueType.class
180369416        8 -rw-r--r--    1 zundel           zundel                733 Jul 13 11:12 .pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/AutoValueMain.class

$ vi examples//src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/AutoValueMain.java
# Comment out the @AutoValue annotation and the contents of the main() entry point

./pants compile examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue --compile-zinc-java-enabled

$ find .pants.d -name '*Auto*.java' -ls
180369414        8 -rw-r--r--    1 zundel           zundel               1024 Jul 13 11:12 .pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/AutoValue_AutoValueMain_ValueType.java
$ find .pants.d -name '*Auto*.class' -ls

180369417        8 -rw-r--r--    1 zundel           zundel               1185 Jul 13 11:12 .pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/AutoValue_AutoValueMain_ValueType.class
180369514        8 -rw-r--r--    1 zundel           zundel                622 Jul 13 11:13 .pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/AutoValueMain$ValueType.class
180369515        8 -rw-r--r--    1 zundel           zundel                413 Jul 13 11:13 .pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/AutoValueMain.class
```

Note that the old generated `AutoValue_AutoValueMain_ValueType.java` and `AutoValue_AutoValueMain_ValueType.class` file is still around.  This may not be a huge problem for auto value, but for some generators is may leave references to other classes that aren't on the classpath.  These are hard for tools like IntelliJ to load.

A potential solution for this problem is to always clean out the isolated classpath output dir ( `pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes/org/pantsbuild/example/autovalue/`  in this case)

issue_title: Shade Cobertura to prevent runtime classpath clashes
issue_body:
Cobertura should be shaded at runtime to prevent it's classpath from clashing with that of the application it's checking - specifically it requires a SLF4J implementation in order to log but forcing an implementation on the user can cause some problems (such as where the user is using a testing implementation of SLF4j).

The Cobertura jar used at runtime should also be changed to `net.sourceforge.cobertura:cobertura-runtime` as this removes a number of unnecessary dependencies.

#4197 and #4198 relate to removing the SLF4J implementation but as a result also remove the ability for Cobertura to log.

issue_title: NailgunExecutor fingerprinting should safeguard the java exe better
issue_body: Right now the java exe path is part of the fingerprint, but a very common case is for that path to be something like `/usr/bin/java` whatever the actual version of java.  This leaves the fingerprinting fooled by a java upgrade that occurs with a nailgun already running on the old java for example.  The fingerprint should be fixed to include the java distribution's version number.

The code of web.run_app does not take into account that the loop could be shared with other tasks or funcitonalities.

issue_title: Allow to mount subapp without prefix
issue_body:
## Long story short

In our project we have two backends: api and web site. Both of them inherits common middleware from base application and have some customization (like default response renderer, session engine, etc).
Only way to implement such architecture is to mount website to /web/ prefix and mobile api to /api/ prefix. But it is more desirable for web site to locate in root url instead of /web/. 

 
       RootApp(common middleware, config, etc)
        /                                  \
     "/web/" (jinja2, cookie auth)         "/api/" (json, token-based auth)

## Expected behaviour

Ability to mount subapp to root url like app.add_subapp('/', subapp)

 
       RootApp(common middleware, config, etc)
        /                                  \
      "/" (jinja2, cookie auth)            "/api/" (json, token-based auth)

issue_title: S3 Put Object Failed with aiohttp==1.1.6
issue_body:
Hi,

I found a problem with using `aiobotocore` with the latest version of `aiohttp`. Apparently, `aiohttp==1.1.6` depends on [`yarl==0.8.1`](https://github.com/aio-libs/yarl/compare/v0.7.1...v0.8.1) or greater. If I downgrade it to `aiohttp==1.1.5` with `yarl==0.7.1`, the problem doesn't occur.

## Problem

If I'm using a parenthesis on `Key` value, it will throw an exception.

## Example

```python
import asyncio, aiobotocore
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
session = aiobotocore.get_session(loop=loop)
s3_client = session.create_client('s3')
s3_bucket = "test-bucket-name"
s3_path = "test-(key).txt"
loop.run_until_complete(s3_client.put_object(Bucket=s3_bucket,Key=s3_path, Body="test"))
```

The code throws:

```
botocore.exceptions.ClientError: An error occurred (SignatureDoesNotMatch) when calling the PutObject operation: The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. Check your key and signing method.
```

However, the following code works properly:

```python
import asyncio, aiobotocore
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
session = aiobotocore.get_session(loop=loop)
s3_client = session.create_client('s3')
s3_bucket = "test-bucket-name"
s3_path = "test-key.txt"
loop.run_until_complete(s3_client.put_object(Bucket=s3_bucket,Key=s3_path, Body="test"))
```

Tested with `aiobotocore==0.0.6` and `botocore==1.4.73`

issue_body: If a user creates a model and passes data into it, there is a period of time where the anomaly likelihood is always 0.5 until it gathers enough data to start it's windowed calculation. If this model is serialized and restarted, this same learning period applies again. Ideally, this resurrected model would retain the anomaly likelihood data so it could continue where it left off instead of starting with a bunch of 0.5 values upon resurrection.

issue_title: Nesting field should validate nested schema instance
issue_body:
Consider the following test cases:


```Python
class Inner(Schema):
    foo = fields.Field()
    bar = fields.Field()
    baz = fields.Field()

def test_nested_schema_instance_only_and_exclude():
    class Outer(Schema):
        inner = fields.Nested(Inner(), only=('foo', 'bar'), exclude=('bar', ))

    sch = Outer()
    data = dict(inner=dict(foo=42, bar=24, baz=242))
    result = sch.dump(data)
    assert 'foo' in result.data['inner']
    assert 'bar' not in result.data['inner']

def test_nested_schema_class_only_and_exclude():
    class Outer(Schema):
        inner = fields.Nested(Inner, only=('foo', 'bar'), exclude=('bar', ))

    sch = Outer()
    data = dict(inner=dict(foo=42, bar=24, baz=242))
    result = sch.dump(data)
    assert 'foo' in result.data['inner']
    assert 'bar' not in result.data['inner']
```

The only difference is `Inner()` vs `Inner`. The first test case fails as `bar` field will be included even though it is passed to the exclusion list of the nesting field.

Thus, when `SchemaABC` instance is passed we should validate `many`, `only`, `exclude` settings and raise an error if something is conflicting.

issue_title: Suggestion: Refactor builtin fields to expose validators for re-use
issue_body:
For my use case, I want to have a custom field-getter using `fields.Function()` but the value it gets will be a UUID. I'd like to be able to have the same validation as the builtin `fields.UUID()`. However, that doesn't seem to be [available in the `validate` module.](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/dev/marshmallow/validate.py)

My suggestion is to refactor all the builtin field types so that they call validators in `validate`. That way, the same validators would also be exposed for reuse in custom user-defined fields.

As I'm imagining this, it would be a fully backwards-compatible change and the only difference to the public interface would be documenting/exposing new validators.

If that sounds like a good idea to the maintainers, I'm happy to take a crack at this and open a PR.

issue_title: Allow for subselects
issue_body:
Hi, 

I've come across an issue with subselects. 

My first problem was that i couldn't use the result of a `_select`  in an equals comparison operator, later i found out i can use `belongs` for that. Now i don't know if that will have performance penalties on some platforms, but i consider it for later concern. 

First and foremost, i need to combine two select statements that are related. 

Consider  the following model: 
```python 
db = DAL('sqlite:memory')
db.define_table('data',Field('effdt','datetime'),Field('v'), Field('s'))
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now(),v=10, s='mine')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now()-timedelta(5),v=5,s='mine')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now()+timedelta(5),v=15,s='mine')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now(),v=10, s='other')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now()-timedelta(5),v=5,s='other')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now()+timedelta(5),v=15,s='other')
```

Now when i try to get the current v for both 'mine' and 'other'  i use the following: 

```python
print('- #0 ----------')
print (db.executesql("""
select effdt, v, s
from data d
where effdt == (
  select max(d_ed.effdt)
  from data d_ed
  where d_ed.effdt <= $NOW
    and d_ed.s = d.s
)
""",dict(NOW=datetime.now())))
```

I've tried to move this into DAL code, and have been struggling with it [for quite some time](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!msg/web2py/ePaW1YEeKFg/hhL9D1iWavoJ). 
Now i came up with this: 

```python
d = db.data.with_alias('d')
d_ed = db.data.with_alias('d_ed')
subselect = db((d_ed.effdt <= datetime.now()) & (d_ed.s == d.s))._select(d_ed.effdt.max())
result = db(d.effdt.belongs(subselect.replace(', data AS d',''))).select(d.effdt, d.v, d.s)
```
which fortunately produces accurate results. 
But i find it quite ugly to patch the subselect string with data that is quite 'woven' with the structure of the query. It's not very 'dynamic'. 

I've tried alternate ways, like using an inner join on a query , but sqlite3 won't allow me to subselect with multiple columns apparently so that was one was off. 

So finally i came up with a little patch, shown below, to allow for an extra parameter to `_select`. Here's a sample for it in use: 

```python
d = db.data.with_alias('d')
d_ed = db.data.with_alias('d_ed')
subselect = db((d_ed.effdt <= datetime.now()) & (d_ed.s == d.s))._select(d_ed.effdt.max(), outer_scoped=[d])
result = db(d.effdt.belongs(subselect)).select(d.effdt, d.v, d.s)
```
As you can see I added `outer_scoped` in which all tables and aliases are placed that need not be mentioned in the `from` clause in the sql statement, because they refer to some name scoped outside of this query.  
Works like a charm. 

I don't have any experience with a pull request yet, but if you'd like i'll give it a try. 

Hoping for this addition to come through, cause it will save me a hell of a lot of hardcoded effective dated queries. 

Thanks. 
PS.: I don't want a workaround as proposed in the groups thread mentioned above. I have queries using tens of these constructs in one single sql statement. Hardcoding those disallows for later addition of web2py/pydal common fields to work without handcrafted maintenance and database independence. 

```diff
Index: /pydal/adapters/base.py
IDEA additional info:
Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP
<+>UTF-8
===================================================================
--- /pydal/adapters/base.py	(revision )
+++ /pydal/adapters/base.py	(revision )
@@ -39,7 +39,7 @@
 SELECT_ARGS = set(
     ('orderby', 'groupby', 'limitby', 'required', 'cache', 'left', 'distinct',
      'having', 'join', 'for_update', 'processor', 'cacheable',
-     'orderby_on_limitby'))
+     'orderby_on_limitby', 'outer_scoped'))
 
 
 class AdapterMeta(type):
@@ -1092,11 +1092,12 @@
         args_get = attributes.get
         tablenames = tables(query)
         tablenames_for_common_filters = tablenames
+        outer_scoped = [t._tablename for t in args_get('outer_scoped',[])]
         for field in fields:
             for tablename in tables(field):
                 if not tablename in tablenames:
                     tablenames.append(tablename)
@@ -1161,6 +1162,8 @@
                         if not t in important_tablenames ]
         else:
             excluded = tablenames
+
+        tablenames = [t for t in tablenames if t not in outer_scoped]
 
         if use_common_filters(query):
             query = self.common_filter(query,tablenames_for_common_filters)

```

issue_title: [FEATURE] Add support for returning indices smaller than `disk_space` in space filter (PR enclosed)
issue_body:
Feature request:
Support returning indices *smaller* than `disk_space` in the space filter.

I needed a good way to use the shrink API on a bunch of different small indices (smaller than `disk_space` setting) that had too many shards. By adding a toggle for the comparison operator in the space filter, I was able to do this. I will add the pull request to this issue.

issue_title: Delete marvel indices automatically
issue_body:
Hi, i installed curator to periodically delete the marvel indices. I've scheduled a windows task that runs every day, which executes a .bat file with the curator code. 
My code for Curator v.2 was this:  
curator delete --older-than 7 --prefix .marvel
Some days ago I upgraded curator to v.3, and i replaced my code with the following: 
curator delete indices --older-than 7 --prefix .marvel --time-unit days --timestring '%Y.%m.%d'

But it doesn't work. I read the documentation and seems that curator auto-prune the marvel indices to prevent accidental deletion.  
This was the reason why i installed curator: to be able to perform the delete-close-bloom-optimize  operations every day automatically, because the marvel data grows every day.

Is there a way to periodically delete the marvel data with curator?

issue_title: about veritesting exploration tech
issue_body:
I read the original veritesting paper and angr implementation and found some (maybe bugs) ?:

1.the veritesting tech ignored some stashes, only the following stashes merged into main simgr:
https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/master/angr/exploration_techniques/veritesting.py#L28
obviously the errored stash is discarded, this may cause some error state not detected by those checkers relying on error stash, maybe deadended and some others should be kept too and let higher level code to drop it or generate testcase?

2.the original paper do veritesting only at symbolic branch starting point, but from the exploration technique code, it seems that veritesting analysis is on at every state-step this may slow the SE noticeabley, maybe it's better to do at sym forking point by hooking 'engine_process'?

3. it seems https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/master/angr/analyses/veritesting.py#L196 is not necessary because https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/master/angr/analyses/veritesting.py#L229 has already done this?

issue_title: Multiple different Problems leading to Symexec .step() running infinite
issue_body:
Hello, 

another day - another headache problem from me. Analyzing a few thousand arm32 files, we stumbled upon an interesting bug which causes the symbolic execution to run infinite with a single `step()`. Sadly, there are multiple problems that lead to this problem.

First the code and the targets:

Starting-Point: `0x7754B9`
Find-Point: `0x007754D4` [JNI-Method]

Setup: 
```python
import logging
logging.basicConfig()
l = logging.getLogger()
l.setLevel(logging.INFO)

import angr

backend = "elf"
binarypath = "933b179091b73a5eb6cbe57639280f4880d3e37eb63f2c295e42f97c22ab70a6"
proj_options = {'auto_load_libs': False, 'main_opts': {'backend': backend}}
project = angr.Project(binarypath, load_options=proj_options, translation_cache = False)

angr_explorer_tech = angr.exploration_techniques.Explorer( \
            find=0x007754d5, \
            find_stash='found', \
            avoid_stash='avoid', \
            num_find=1)

state = project.factory.blank_state(addr=0x7754B9)
simgr = project.factory.simgr([state])
simgr.use_technique(angr_explorer_tech)
simgr.step()
``` 

![ida](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12801920/42690540-dc766186-86a4-11e8-95ae-842a7c4b8c7f.png)

```
.text:007754B8 ; djinni::Optional<std::experimental::optional, djinni::Bool>::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *, std::experimental::optional<bool> const&)
.text:007754B8                 WEAK _ZN6djinni8OptionalINSt12experimental8optionalENS_4BoolEE7fromCppEP7_JNIEnvRKNS2_IbEE
.text:007754B8 _ZN6djinni8OptionalINSt12experimental8optionalENS_4BoolEE7fromCppEP7_JNIEnvRKNS2_IbEE
.text:007754B8                                         ; CODE XREF: djinni_generated::NativeDbxExtendedFixedPhotoMetadata::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *,dropbox::product::dbapp::camera_upload::cu_engine::DbxExtendedFixedPhotoMetadata const&)+44↓p
.text:007754B8                                         ; djinni_generated::NativeDbxExtendedFixedPhotoMetadata::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *,dropbox::product::dbapp::camera_upload::cu_engine::DbxExtendedFixedPhotoMetadata const&)+54↓p ...
.text:007754B8                 PUSH            {R4-R6,LR}
.text:007754BA                 MOV             R4, R0
.text:007754BC                 LDRB            R3, [R2]
.text:007754BE                 MOV             R5, R1
.text:007754C0                 MOV             R6, R2
.text:007754C2                 CBZ             R3, loc_7754E4

.text:007754C4                 LDR             R3, =(loc_88D50C - 0x3754CC)
.text:007754C6                 MOV             R0, R1
.text:007754C8                 ADD             R3, PC  ; _ZN6djinni8JniClassINS_4BoolEE11s_singletonE_ptr
.text:007754CA                 LDR             R3, [R3] ; djinni::JniClass<djinni::Bool>::s_singleton
.text:007754CC                 LDR             R3, [R3] ; djinni::JniClass<djinni::Bool>::s_singleton
.text:007754CE                 LDMIA.W         R3, {R1,R2}
.text:007754D2                 LDRB            R3, [R6,#1]
.text:007754D4                 BL              _ZN7_JNIEnv22CallStaticObjectMethodEP7_jclassP10_jmethodIDz ; 
   ...
   ...
.text:007754E4 loc_7754E4                              ; CODE XREF: djinni::Optional<std::experimental::optional,djinni::Bool>::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *,std::experimental::optional<bool> const&)+A↑j
.text:007754E4                 STR             R3, [R0]
.text:007754E6
.text:007754E6 loc_7754E6                              ; CODE XREF: djinni::Optional<std::experimental::optional,djinni::Bool>::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *,std::experimental::optional<bool> const&)+2A↑j
.text:007754E6                 MOV             R0, R4
.text:007754E8                 POP             {R4-R6,PC}

..
...
0x76C772 ; _JNIEnv::CallStaticObjectMethod(_jclass *, _jmethodID *, ...)
```

Problem 1: Exception in explorer, no successors:
```
Replacing explorer.py :: 127
state = self.project.factory.successors(state, num_inst=1).successors[0]

With:
succs = self.project.factory.successors(state, num_inst=1)
                    try:
                        if succs.successors:
                            state = succs.successors[0]
                    except SimIRSBNoDecodeError as ex:
                        if state.arch.name.startswith('MIPS'): 
                        ...
```

The solution may not be handy or sound, even faulty - but it brings the real problem to the top.


```python
Before Step: Active -> [0x7754B9]
After-Step: 
expected: active -> [0x7754E5, 0x7754C5] (At branching location, fallthrough branching)
faulty: active -> [0x7754E5, 0x76C773]
```
The step function at this point executes the entire basic block (which has incorrect boundaries, e.g. does not stop block at `CBZ`. While executing until the `BL` not stoping at `CBZ` it also ignores the `find addr`. Even more problematic, it also ignores setting the `LR` at `BL`. Hitting the return in the successive blocks it starts again at the beginning, thus leading to an un-interruptable infinite loop with a single `step()`.   

The same behaviour can be seen at `CBNZ` as well. 

Binary:
[933b179091b73a5eb6cbe57639280f4880d3e37eb63f2c295e42f97c22ab70a6.zip](https://github.com/angr/angr/files/2192463/933b179091b73a5eb6cbe57639280f4880d3e37eb63f2c295e42f97c22ab70a6.zip)

Any-Ideas?

Best reagrds,
Andreas

issue_title: Behaviour when both workflow's step and its tool define a default value for an input
issue_body:
In the case where both a workflow defines a default value and the tool in the step also defines one, cwltool picks the default value in the tool.

I haven't seen this behaviour been defined anywhere, and I would expect the values in the tool to be overridden since this is the only way I've seen to 'hardcode' values in workflows, but I don't know if that makes sense for everybody.

For example:
workflow.cwl
```
[...]
steps:
  step:
    run: tool.cwl
    in:
      parameter:
        default: 'workflow'
[...]
```

```
[...]
baseCommand: echo
inputs:
  parameter:
    type: string
    default: tool
```

This would print `tool`, I'd expect it to print `workflow`.

Is this something expected? I can produce conformity tests and tests for cwltool if necessary.

issue_title: WebSearch: support new syntax collection:"CERN Divisions"
issue_body:
Originally on 2012-06-27

`collection:FOO` query is currently mapped onto `980:FOO` query, basically.  For faceted search, it would be desirable if one can pass `collection:"CERN Divisions"`.

This can be achieved by enriching `search_unit()` in a similar way how e.g. `citedby` special queries are done.  The above query would simply return hitset of records belonging to the "CERN Divisions" collection.

Note that there may be a mixup of `collection` concept being used to represent 980 and being used to represent the collection name.  So beware WRT webcoll.  Either we could return Boolean OR between old-behaviour and new-behaviour, or we could distinguish between these cases via two independent `collection` and `collectionname` search operators.

As usual, test cases should be written.

issue_title: signals: "file_downloaded" sent too early
issue_body:
The `file_downloaded` signal [is sent too early during the file download request](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-files-rest/blob/94214019b8b7405067555cb34ab11f051a28bb3d/invenio_files_rest/views.py#L597). This is bad for e.g. `invenio-stats`, which relies on the signal for gathering file download statistics. In case the download is interrupted for some reason (e.g. connection dropped or the user canceled the download), the signal will already have been sent.

A possible solution is to use the [`flask.after_this_request` callback](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/1.0/patterns/deferredcallbacks/), and send the signal afterward if the request was successful (i.e. 2xx/3xx).

issue_title: Running into some resource allocation problems
issue_body:
Since there is no general help forum for Toil or Toil scripts and it is in heavy development, I thought this would be the best place to ask. I have been working on porting over my existing pipeline work to Toil due to its broad support for Mesos. I am currently running this pipeline just locally. I wanted to do something a little different because I re-use many of the same methods in different pipelines so all of the methods live in their own project and are then imported into the pipeline script where I construct the workflow and graph using wrapJobFn() to define all of the jobs and then use the addJob() method to chain them together for maximum flexibility. You can see this in this repo: https://github.com/dgaston/ddbio-ngsflow.

All of my wrapper methods are in ngsflow and the workflow-* scripts are the various pipelines. workflow-somatic-amplicon.py is the current one I am testing.

The problem I am having is that the jobs don't seem to be respecting the resource allocations. For instance I am specifying --maxCores of 4, in my configuration input I specify that BWA should use 4 cores and so that is passed to the cores keyword argument in wrapJobFn(). However when executing I have four different BWA jobs, each multithreaded to 4 threads running (4 different samples) as well as two other jobs executing. It was my understanding that in this case there should only be a single BWA job executing at a time, as each BWA job should be requesting 4 cores out of a max of 4 cores allowed to toil. I see similar issues with memory usage.

Any help would be appreciated.

issue_title: Provide a base class for Error/Exception classes
issue_body:
When writing a try-except block to catch this library's exceptions, one has to add extra lines to catch everything:

```
    try:
        # make calls to fastly   
    except (
        fastly.errors.AuthenticationError,
        fastly.errors.InternalServerError,
        fastly.errors.BadRequestError,
        fastly.errors.NotFoundError
    ) as e:
        # handle exception
```

It would be nice if the error classes shared the same base class so that consumers only had to catch a single exception:

```
    try:
        # make calls to fastly   
    except fastly.errors.FastlyError as e:
        # handle exception
```

issue_title: Support using existing Docker images as hooks
issue_body:
Currently a `language: docker` hook only supports running a Docker image which it built itself (via a `Dockerfile`).

However this seems a little unnecessary and expensive since we can and should be building and publishing our Docker images to a registry.

Would there be interest in supporting using existing images as hooks? It should be simple and I'd be happy to make the PR.

## Proposed `.pre-commit-hooks.yaml` change

```yaml
-   id: flake8-docker
    name: flake8 (via docker)
    description: flake8, but provided by docker
    files: \.py$

    language: docker
    # Image to `docker pull`
    docker_image: my.registry.example.com/pre-commit-hooks/flake8:latest
    # Optional: use `ENTRYPOINT` of the image if not specified
    entry: flake8
```

issue_title: Isolate test environment
issue_body:
```shell-session
$ sudo apt install python-tox
$ cd pre-commit-hooks
$ pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
$ make test
```

gives the following output: https://gist.github.com/nagromc/8d44fa7c3fcddcc0af35fe5d9f789c70#file-make-test-output-txt

After some investigation, I found out my Git configuration was interfering with the tests.

I think the tests should be independent from the environment either by:
- running `env HOME=/tmp make test` (to change temporarily the `HOME` and then ignore my existing Git configuration `~/.gitconfig`);
- or doing a (Python|Docker|Vagrant) trick to isolate the test environment.

issue_title: How to go backwards from embedding to token/character space?

issue_title: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED]
issue_body:
Be sure to check the existing issues (both open and closed!).

Describe the issue briefly here.

Since updating to 10.11.0 Pipenv always fails when creating lock file when downloading its own dependencies (what for?)

Please run `$ python -m pipenv.help`, and paste the results here.

Will not do this, this contains too much private information.

OS is MS Windows, Python is 3.6.5, CPython, official build.

------------

##### Expected result

Lock file created.

##### Actual result

```
INFO:pip9._vendor.requests.packages.urllib3.connectionpool:Starting new HTTPS connection (1): pypi.python.org
INFO:pip9._vendor.requests.packages.urllib3.connectionpool:Starting new HTTPS connection (1): pypi.org
INFO:notpip.index:Could not fetch URL https://pypi.python.org/simple/autopep8/: There was a problem confirming the ssl certificate: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:833) - skipping
Warning: Your dependencies could not be resolved. You likely have a mismatch in your sub-dependencies.
  You can use $ pipenv install --skip-lock to bypass this mechanism, then run $ pipenv graph to inspect the situation.
Could not find a version that matches autopep8
Tried: (no version found at all)
```

1. This should be an error, not a warning (Pipenv failed to do everything it was asked, how is this a warning?)
2. What changed? Why are there two different PEM files in Pipenv sources and ssl.py is still using neither? What were you trying to achieve by manipulating these certificates?

##### Steps to replicate

I haven't yet seen any Pipenv version 10.11.0 that would work on MS Windows... just run it on one?

The problem doesn't exist on Linux with the same versions. Bare PIP install works, the URL it is trying to access is accessible to every HTTP client I tried (a bunch of browsers, Cygwin version of curl and wget, Putty's telnet).  I'm still debugging ssl.py to try to get a better error report, but this is really difficult due to how pipenv's code is using it, so I may not get better info.
